english: stated to be a Lama who instructs by word of mouth Mil.; esp with regard to the spelling of words: brda (brda) yaṅ (yang) mi (mi) ₒdra ('dra) sna-tsogs (sna tshogs) gyur (gyur) there came also into use various spellings Zam.; brda-rnyiṅ (brda rnying) old orthography, brda-gsár (brda gsar) new orthography Zam.; bód-kyi (bod kyi) brdai (brda'i) bstan-bcós (bstan bcos) title of the Zamatog; tsig-brda (tshig brda) = tsig (tshig), tsig-brda-yis (tshig brda yis) ₒgrol-ba ('grol ba) to explain by words Mil. Comp. brdá-skad (brda skad) language by symbolical signs Mil.; prob. also nothing but the usual language by words Glr. — brda-čád (brda bcad) (prob. for ₒčad ('chad), from ₒčád-pa ('chad pa) II.), me-loṅ-gi (me long gi) brda-čád (brda bcad) the language or evidence of the mirror; so prob. also Tar. 210, 22. — brda-spród (brda sprod), brda-sbyór (brda sbyor) 1. explanation, miṅ-dón (ming don) brda-spród (brda sprod) explanation of the import of names, title of a small Materia Medica by a certain Wairocana. 2. orthography Gram., Pth. — brda-lon (brda lon) Mil. is said to be = tsig-lan (tshig lan), verbal answer. — brda-lags (brda lags) 'insignis', acc. to Cs. in Journ. As. Soc. Beng. V, 384.
1. Lex. w.e.; Sch. to deceive, to cheat. 2. Sch. to swing, brandish, flourish, γyáb-mo (g.yab mo) a fly-flap.
to slip, to slide, to lose one's footing.
Sch.: mdún-du (mdun du) bsdár-ba (bsdar ba) to hope, to expect or wait for a favour. In Dzl. 22, 18 the better reading (accordant with the manuscript of Kyelang) is sdur (sdur) (=sdú-bar (sdu bar)).

the Lexx. add: grabs (grabs), Cs. to compose, prepare, make ready, nyer (nyer) bsdógs-pa (bsdogs pa) id.; sna-tág (sna thag) bsdógs-pa (bsdogs pa) to wind the rope, which is fastened in the nose of an ox or a camel, round the horns or the neck of the animal.
meadow, C. also ná-ma (na ma); nar (nar) skye (skye) it grows on meadows, Wdṅ. and elsewh. (cf. neu (ne'u)).

I. sbst. 1. year (?) v. na-niṅ (na ning). — 2. stage of life, age, also na-tsód (na tshod), and ná-so (na so), resp. sku-ná (sku na) (also sku-nás (sku nas)?); na-tsód (na tshod) rgyás-pas (rgyas pas) Wdṅ. old, of an advanced age; ná-so (na so) γzhón-te (gzhon te) Glr. young; sku-nás (sku nas) prá-mo (phra mo) Mil. of a tender age; na-tsód-kyi (na tshod kyi) dbyé-ba (dbye ba) the different ages or stages of life; (sku-) nár-son-pa (sku nar son pa) (Sch. grown old?) Glr.: of full age, adult, grown up; *ná-so-tsir-la* (na so tshir la) W. according to age; na-čuṅ (na chung) girl, maiden, virgin, na-čuṅ (na chung) bzáṅ-mo (bzang mo) bču (bcu) ten beautiful girls Dzl.; na-mnyám (na mnyam), -ₒdrá ('dra), -zlá (zla), ncu-ldáṅ (ncu ldan) Lex. of the same age, coetaneous; *ná-da-tom-mo* (na da tom mo) C. a festivity given by wealthy parents on their son's birthday to him and his playmates, also *ló-da-tom-mo* (lo da tom mo); na-prá (na phra) young, tender; na-γzhón (na gzhon) = γzhón-nu (gzhon nu). II. postp. c. accus., signifying the place where a thing is, 1. added to substantives, in, (more accurately náṅ-na (nang na) c. genit.), sometimes also to be rendered by on, at, with, to etc. mdó-na (mdo na) in scripture, lo-rgyús-na (lo rgyus na) in a book of history Glr.; dé-na (de na) there, in that place; of time: dus-γcíg-na (dus gcig na) at the same time, dei (de'i) tsé-na (tshe na) at that time, then etc. — 2. added to verbs, either to the inf., or more frq. (col. always) to the verbal root: in, at, during (the doing or happening of a thing), hence a. when, at the time of, bós-na (bos na) when I called Dzl., zér-ba-na (zer ba na) when he said Tar.; bdág-gi (bdag gi) pa (pha) tse (tshe) pós-na (phos na) when my father shall have died Dzl.; with nam (nam): nam (nam) dús-la (dus la) báb-na (bab na) ( W. *dus léb-na* (dus leb na) ) when the time comes, <
